message: Hey everyone. I'm pumped today about our flash sale
You know it's been 56 years since the Beatles released Abbey Road right? My favourite album
56 years already?? Wow that makes me feel old...
Indeed! We're celebrating today by promoting a flash sale on retro items from 1969

Welcome to Bezs Beatles Day, remember communication is key to diagnose the problem.

 *Access game links by pressing the Uptime Labs Logo in the top right of the screen*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
We're a step closer to allowing folks to pay for items from the 1960s with Bitcoin thanks to Daniel!
Think of it like time travelling!
Wow good work Daniel! Looking forward to seeing it in action in prod
How's the test-driven development going?
TDDs going well. We are as solid as a rock! Though don't ask me what the Liskov Substitution Principle is...
erm - funny you should say that Daniel, I'm just testing my checkout service change in prod and I can't complete the payment - something's up
I hope my checkout service change is all good. Can someone check its not just me? And before you ask, yes I've cleared my cookies
Sorry Daniel it's not looking good on my end either
Uh oh...here we go again
@kdanjo-bloomberg.net can you "Size up the Issue" please and keep us informed
From what I can see it looks like we are still getting orders through
Get this sorted ASAP!!!
HI @tinus, taking a look right now
We're seeing a significant spike in payment failures—about 40% of customer checkouts are not completing successfully right now.

I have just tried to check out a pair a sunglasses and have received the error 'Payment Unavailable
Sorry for the inconvenience, but we're currently unable to process your payment right now.'
:star: Reproduces the incident
Just got a message from my cousin in London, she can't buy any of the new Beatles merch either. Says her payment failed.
I can take over as Incident Management
:star: Take command
I'm noticing the failures seem concentrated in Europe. Looks like the UK is particularly affected.
I will start checking the logs now
Could it be your checkout change again @Shay? It broke the platform last week!
@bob Are customers complaints coming in?
The checkout service logs are healthy and the service is working as intended
I see 504 HTTP errors in the payment service, which means the application is failing to serve the requests
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
@bob If so, are payments failing only for a particular currency?
I'm seeing a spike in latency and the payment service health check is returning 504s, even though CPU and memory usage are normal.
Yes, customer complaints are coming in steadily, especially from the UK where payment failures are most frequent.
The failures are mostly affecting GBP transactions, which aligns with the spike in UK customer complaints.
I have just tried in Japanese Yen and that has gone through
:star: Attempt to replicate the issue
The 504 errors in the payment service are only appearing for UK transaction IDs, and this started just after the recent deployment.
Resource usage remains normal, but the payment service's latency is still elevated and health checks continue to fail with 504s.
The payments in euro work for me as well - so @bob what you're hearing from clients that it's mainly UK seems to be correct
Given the timing and regional impact, we should review any recent code changes that could affect UK payment routing or configuration.
The pattern of 504 errors is consistent for UK transactions and began immediately after Daniel's deployment.
@daniel @shay Can we reverse the deployment? Was that deployment made to the PaymentServices?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
My code isn't even being called yet, so I don't think rolling back is necessary.
Would make more sense to investigate Shay's change first?
@daniel Are there any risks if we do roll back?
Daniel has a point - let's try Shay's change first before touching the payment service
Rolling back my deployment should be low risk since the new code paths are feature-flagged off, but we should still monitor for any unexpected side effects.
@shay Lets revert changes made to CheckoutService. Are there any risks?
I will rollback my checkout change right away
Rollback complete!
Hmm, customer reports are still coming in at the same rate
The failure pattern hasn't changed - still 100% failure in the UK
Something else must be causing this
It if still failing for me too
We need this fixed!
@daniel lets roll back changes to PaymentServices as we are still seeing errors
The new code isn't being invoked yet, can I investigate a bit more.
Are there any risks to reverting now?
if not let's try
Fine if you insist, I'll rollback
Rolling back my Bitcoin payment service change now
Rollback complete. Might take a few minutes to stabilise
Did that fix it?
The rollback is already done and should be safe, but let's confirm everything is working as expected.
Payments in GBP are working for me now
:star: Validate the fix
Would you like me to try placing a test order to check if the payment issue is resolved?
@bob any reports from clients?
That's great news—sounds like the rollback did the trick!
Looking into it now... Metrics seem to be improving!
:star: Cross check resolution with stakeholders
Customer payments appear to be back up and processing normally
yes please @tanya
Excellent
I'll check the payment service logs now.
I'll try a test transaction from my side.
I don't think the Beatles would've anticipated cryptocurrency so let's keep it that way for now
Logs are looking clean, no more 504 errors.
